title: Unlocking Operational Transfer Pricing potential - Deloitte
summary: Unlocking Operational Transfer Pricing potential Deloitte
ai_summary: This Deloitte article explores the strategic importance and practical implementation of Operational Transfer Pricing (OTP). It discusses how integrating TP into daily business operations can enhance compliance, efficiency, and risk management for multinational enterprises.

title: Only Solar Days Count for Determining PE Under India-Saudi Arabia DTAA: ITAT Bangalore
summary: Bangalore ITAT ruled that only solar days and not cumulative man-days should be considered while determining the existence of a Permanent Establishment under the India-Saudi Arabia DTAA. Since the assessee’s stay was only 90 days, no PE was held to exist in India. The post Only Solar Days Count for Determining PE Under India-Saudi Arabia DTAA: ITAT Bangalore appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Bangalore ruled that only solar days, not cumulative man-days, should be considered for determining a Permanent Establishment under the India-Saudi Arabia DTAA. This decision meant no PE existed in India as the assessee's stay was only 90 days.

title: ITAT Mumbai Deletes Tax Addition Since UAE Consultancy Firm Had No PE in India
summary: Mumbai ITAT held that business receipts from an Indian associated enterprise were not taxable in India because the assessee had no Permanent Establishment under the India-UAE DTAA. The Tribunal ruled that mere provision of personnel did not automatically create a PE. The post ITAT Mumbai Deletes Tax Addition Since UAE Consultancy Firm Had No PE in India appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Mumbai deleted a tax addition, holding that a UAE consultancy firm had no Permanent Establishment in India under the India-UAE DTAA. The Tribunal clarified that the mere provision of personnel did not automatically establish a PE for tax purposes.

title: AO Cannot Replace DCF Method With NAV Method for Share Valuation under Rule 11UA: ITAT Mumbai
summary: The Tribunal ruled that once an assessee validly opts for the DCF method and submits a qualified valuation report, the Assessing Officer cannot switch to the NAV method. The decision reinforces the statutory valuation options available under Rule 11UA. The post AO Cannot Replace DCF Method With NAV Method for Share Valuation under Rule 11UA: ITAT Mumbai appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Mumbai ruled that an Assessing Officer cannot replace the Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) method with the Net Asset Value (NAV) method for share valuation under Rule 11UA if the assessee validly opts for DCF. This decision reinforces the taxpayer's choice of statutory valuation methods for unquoted shares.

title: Concessional 22% Corporate Tax Rate Cannot Be Denied for Procedural Lapse: Rajasthan HC
summary: The Rajasthan High Court held that the benefit of Section 115BAA could not be denied when Form 10-IC was filed within the period permitted under CBDT Circulars. The Court ruled that procedural delay could not defeat a clearly exercised tax option. The post Concessional 22% Corporate Tax Rate Cannot Be Denied for Procedural Lapse: Rajasthan HC appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Rajasthan High Court held that the benefit of Section 115BAA for a concessional 22% corporate tax rate could not be denied for procedural lapse if Form 10-IC was filed within the CBDT Circulars' permitted period. The Court ruled that procedural delay should not defeat a clearly exercised tax option.

title: DVO Valuation Alone Cannot Justify Section 69 Addition Without Extra Payment Proof: ITAT Amritsar
summary: The ITAT Amritsar held that a valuation report by itself cannot justify addition under Section 69 without evidence of extra payment. The Tribunal deleted the addition after finding no proof of on-money beyond the registered sale deed value. The post DVO Valuation Alone Cannot Justify Section 69 Addition Without Extra Payment Proof: ITAT Amritsar appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Amritsar held that a Departmental Valuation Officer's (DVO) report alone cannot justify an addition under Section 69 without corroborating evidence of extra payment. The Tribunal deleted the addition, finding no proof of on-money beyond the registered sale deed value.

title: Competitive Corporate Tax Policy Is Essential for European Economic Growth
summary: Debates over tax fairness and anti-avoidance rules have dominated European tax policy circles for over a decade. However, as geoeconomic pressures increase around the world, policymakers are looking for ways to boost European competitiveness and economic growth.
ai_summary: The article discusses the broader debate surrounding corporate tax policy and anti-avoidance rules in Europe, emphasizing the need for competitive tax policies to foster economic growth amidst global geoeconomic pressures. It highlights a shift in focus among European policymakers towards boosting competitiveness rather than solely concentrating on tax fairness debates.

title: ITAT Deletes Section 43CA Addition as DVO Valuation Difference Was Within 10% Safe Harbour Limit
summary: Mumbai ITAT held that no addition under Section 43CA could survive where difference between agreement value and DVO valuation remained within 10% tolerance band. Tribunal ruled that safe harbour benefit applies once DVO valuation substitutes stamp duty value. The post ITAT Deletes Section 43CA Addition as DVO Valuation Difference Was Within 10% Safe Harbour Limit appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Mumbai ITAT ruled that no addition under Section 43CA could be made where the difference between the agreement value and the DVO valuation for immovable property was within the 10% safe harbour limit. The Tribunal affirmed that the safe harbour benefit applies once the DVO valuation replaces the stamp duty value.

title: Stamp Duty Value Cannot Be Adopted Without DVO Reference When Property Title Is Disputed
summary: Tribunal ruled that objections relating to defective title, encroachments, and legal disputes require proper valuation examination through a DVO reference. The addition under Section 56(2)(x) was therefore restored to the Assessing Officer for reconsideration. The post Stamp Duty Value Cannot Be Adopted Without DVO Reference When Property Title Is Disputed appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Tribunal ruled that the stamp duty value of property cannot be adopted without referring the matter to a Departmental Valuation Officer (DVO) when the property title is disputed due to defects or legal issues. Consequently, an addition under Section 56(2)(x) was remitted to the Assessing Officer for reconsideration.

title: AO Must Refer Property Valuation to DVO When Stamp Duty Value Is Disputed: ITAT Delhi
summary: ITAT Delhi ruled that where an assessee disputes the stamp duty valuation under Section 50C, the Assessing Officer should refer the matter to the Valuation Officer. The Tribunal set aside the capital gains addition for fresh determination. The post AO Must Refer Property Valuation to DVO When Stamp Duty Value Is Disputed: ITAT Delhi appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Delhi ruled that if an assessee disputes the stamp duty valuation under Section 50C for immovable property, the Assessing Officer must refer the valuation to a Departmental Valuation Officer (DVO). The Tribunal set aside the capital gains addition, directing a fresh determination based on proper valuation.

title: Gulf Instability Tests Multinationals’ Transfer Pricing Policies
summary: Gulf Instability Tests Multinationals’ Transfer Pricing Policies news.bloombergtax.com
ai_summary: This article examines how geopolitical instability in the Gulf region is currently challenging existing transfer pricing policies for multinational corporations. Such instability often compels MNEs to reassess supply chains, operational models, and ultimately, their intercompany pricing strategies, presenting notable practitioner analysis on risk management.

title: Swedish Court Annuls Power Deal Transfer Pricing Adjustment (1)
summary: Swedish Court Annuls Power Deal Transfer Pricing Adjustment (1) news.bloombergtax.com
ai_summary: A Swedish court has reportedly annulled a transfer pricing adjustment related to a power deal, marking a significant legal development. This ruling provides important insights into judicial interpretations of arm's length principles within the energy sector in Sweden.

title: Huge Turnover Difference Between Taxpayer, Comparable Warrants Exclusion In Transfer Pricing Analysis:... - LiveLawBiz
summary: Huge Turnover Difference Between Taxpayer, Comparable Warrants Exclusion In Transfer Pricing Analysis:... LiveLawBiz
ai_summary: An unnamed court or tribunal has ruled that a substantial turnover difference between a taxpayer and a potential comparable warrants the exclusion of that comparable from the transfer pricing analysis. This significant decision reinforces the importance of stringent comparability criteria in applying the arm's length methodology.

title: AI and data sharing fuel rise in African tax disputes - Moneyweb
summary: AI and data sharing fuel rise in African tax disputes Moneyweb
ai_summary: The rise of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and extensive data sharing is increasingly fueling tax disputes across Africa. These notable disputes likely include complex transfer pricing challenges related to the valuation and attribution of profits from digital intangibles and data, highlighting emerging issues in the digital economy.

title: Global Brand & Technology Support Payments Not Taxable as Royalty Under India-UK DTAA
summary: ITAT Mumbai ruled that payments made for global brand, communications, and technology support services within the Deloitte network did not amount to royalty under Article 13(3) of the India-UK DTAA. The Tribunal held that no transfer of copyright or intellectual property rights had taken place. The post Global Brand & Technology Support Payments Not Taxable as Royalty Under India-UK DTAA appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Mumbai ruled that payments for global brand, communications, and technology support services within the Deloitte network are not taxable as royalty under Article 13(3) of the India-UK DTAA. This significant decision found no transfer of copyright or intellectual property rights, thereby impacting the characterization of intercompany charges related to intangibles.

title: Reversal of Previously Disallowed Securitisation Provisions Cannot Be Taxed Again: ITAT Mumbai
summary: The Mumbai ITAT held that reversal of securitisation provisions already disallowed in earlier years cannot be taxed again upon write-back. The Tribunal ruled that such taxation would amount to double taxation. The post Reversal of Previously Disallowed Securitisation Provisions Cannot Be Taxed Again: ITAT Mumbai appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Mumbai ITAT ruled that the reversal of securitisation provisions previously disallowed in earlier assessment years cannot be taxed again upon write-back. The Tribunal emphasized that taxing such reversals would lead to double taxation under domestic tax law, without direct transfer pricing implications.

title: Difference Between Projected & Actual Revenue Cannot Invalidate DCF Valuation: ITAT Delhi
summary: Tribunal ruled that future projections under DCF method cannot be tested solely against later actual financial performance. It observed that valuation is based on assumptions and future business expectations prevailing on valuation date. The post Difference Between Projected & Actual Revenue Cannot Invalidate DCF Valuation: ITAT Delhi appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Delhi ruled that a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) valuation method cannot be invalidated solely based on differences between projected and actual future revenue outcomes. This significant decision emphasizes that valuations are based on assumptions and business expectations prevailing at the valuation date, not hindsight, reinforcing principles crucial for valuation of intra-group transactions.

title: Land Acquisition Compensation Cannot Be Subjected to TDS as It Arises From Compulsory Acquisition
summary: Karnataka High Court directed that compensation paid under a mediated land acquisition settlement must be released without deduction of TDS. The ruling relied on an earlier High Court decision concerning compulsory acquisition compensation. The post Land Acquisition Compensation Cannot Be Subjected to TDS as It Arises From Compulsory Acquisition appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Karnataka High Court ruled that compensation paid for land acquisition, especially under a mediated settlement, cannot be subjected to Tax Deducted at Source (TDS) as it arises from compulsory acquisition. This decision reinforces an earlier High Court ruling regarding the exemption of such compensation from TDS, which is a domestic tax compliance matter.

title: June 16, 2026 | Global Asset Management Tax Webcast: A Focus on Transfer Pricing - KPMG
summary: June 16, 2026 | Global Asset Management Tax Webcast: A Focus on Transfer Pricing KPMG
ai_summary: KPMG is hosting a global webcast on June 16, 2026, specifically focusing on transfer pricing issues within the asset management industry. This event indicates practitioner interest in navigating the complex TP challenges for multinational asset managers.

title: ECJ Case: Intra-group transfer pricing adjustments and VAT treatment - Global VAT Compliance
summary: ECJ Case: Intra-group transfer pricing adjustments and VAT treatment Global VAT Compliance
ai_summary: A new European Court of Justice (ECJ) case is examining the intricate relationship between intra-group transfer pricing adjustments and their corresponding VAT treatment across EU member states. This ruling will provide crucial guidance on how changes in taxable value due to TP adjustments impact VAT obligations.

title: Navigating permanent establishment risk in a remote work era: the OECD’s 2025 Commentary and local approaches - KPMG
summary: Navigating permanent establishment risk in a remote work era: the OECD’s 2025 Commentary and local approaches KPMG
ai_summary: KPMG is analyzing the implications of the OECD’s 2025 Commentary on permanent establishment (PE) risks, particularly concerning the evolving remote work landscape. This analysis highlights the need for multinationals to understand both global guidance and varied local approaches to PE attribution in the context of distributed workforces.

title: Bangalore ITAT Grants Relief in Section 50C Addition – DVO Valuation in Co-Owner’s Case Accepted Over Stamp Duty Value
summary: The ITAT ruled that the Assessing Officer wrongly adopted the stamp duty valuation despite contrary valuation material on record. The Tribunal directed fresh capital gain computation using the lower departmental valuation. The post Bangalore ITAT Grants Relief in Section 50C Addition – DVO Valuation in Co-Owner’s Case Accepted Over Stamp Duty Value appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Bangalore ITAT ruled against the Assessing Officer's adoption of stamp duty valuation for Section 50C addition, accepting the Departmental Valuation Officer's (DVO) report instead. This decision provides relief in capital gains computation, emphasizing the consideration of all relevant valuation material.

title: ITAT Deletes Addition Because Stamp Duty Value Ignored Illegal Construction
summary: The ITAT held that stamp duty valuation could not be blindly adopted where the property was affected by BBMP demolition proceedings for unauthorized construction. The Tribunal accepted the actual purchase price as fair market value and deleted the addition. The post ITAT Deletes Addition Because Stamp Duty Value Ignored Illegal Construction appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT held that stamp duty valuation cannot be blindly applied when a property's value is significantly impacted by factors like illegal construction and demolition proceedings. The Tribunal accepted the actual purchase price as the fair market value, leading to the deletion of a tax addition.

title: Bangalore ITAT Slashes Estimated Profit from 15% to 7% – Best Judgment Assessment Cannot Be a “Blind Guess”
summary: The Tribunal ruled that a flat 15% profit estimation was excessive where all contract receipts were received through banking channels with TDS deductions. It directed recomputation of income at 7% of turnover. The post Bangalore ITAT Slashes Estimated Profit from 15% to 7% – Best Judgment Assessment Cannot Be a “Blind Guess” appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Bangalore ITAT significantly reduced a profit estimation from 15% to 7% of turnover, ruling that best judgment assessments cannot be arbitrary. This decision underscores the need for reasoned estimations, especially where receipts are accounted for via banking channels with TDS.

title: EY talks tax: EY Tax.Tech™ 2026: Building the future of Tax - AI in tax - what's working now and what's next (May 6, 2026)
summary: In this episode, panelists focus on current applications of artificial intelligence (AI) in tax, providing practical examples of how AI assists with core tax processes, how today's capabilities differ meaningfully from those available even two years ago, and considerations for moving from pilot efforts to production.
ai_summary: EY's Tax.Tech™ 2026 episode explores current and future applications of artificial intelligence (AI) in the tax function, detailing practical examples of its use in core tax processes. The discussion highlights the rapid advancements in AI capabilities and considerations for adopting AI from pilot projects to full production in tax operations.

title: Clarification of the terms “Director” and “Officer” under Article 36 of the UAE Corporate Tax Law - KPMG
summary: Clarification of the terms “Director” and “Officer” under Article 36 of the UAE Corporate Tax Law KPMG
ai_summary: KPMG provided a clarification on the terms "Director" and "Officer" as defined under Article 36 of the UAE Corporate Tax Law. title: ITAT Grants Reckitt Relief in Marketing Intangibles Transfer Pricing Dispute - Studycafe
summary: ITAT Grants Reckitt Relief in Marketing Intangibles Transfer Pricing Dispute Studycafe
ai_summary: The Indian Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) provided relief to Reckitt in a transfer pricing dispute concerning marketing intangibles. This ruling addresses the arm's length valuation and attribution of profits related to brand promotion expenses incurred by the Indian entity.

title: CPC Cannot Change AOP Status to Co-operative Society in ITR Processing: ITAT Ahmedabad
summary: ITAT Ahmedabad held that CPC cannot suo motu reclassify an apartment owners association as a co-operative society while processing returns under Section 143(1). The Tribunal deleted the Rs. 97,690 demand after finding the adjustment beyond the scope of prima facie processing. The post CPC Cannot Change AOP Status to Co-operative Society in ITR Processing: ITAT Ahmedabad appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Ahmedabad ruled that the CPC cannot suo motu change the status of an Association of Persons (AOP) to a co-operative society during income tax return processing under Section 143(1). The Tribunal deleted a demand of Rs. 97,690, deeming the adjustment outside the scope of prima facie processing.

title: India’s TP reset for the digital economy: safe harbour and APA reforms - International Tax Review
summary: India’s TP reset for the digital economy: safe harbour and APA reforms International Tax Review
ai_summary: This article examines India's comprehensive overhaul of its transfer pricing framework concerning the digital economy. It specifically addresses anticipated reforms to safe harbour provisions and the Advance Pricing Agreement (APA) program to align with the evolving digital landscape.

title: AO Cannot Apply Post-2018 “Look-Through” Valuation Retrospectively – ITAT Deletes Massive Section 56 Additions
summary: ITAT Mumbai deleted additions exceeding ₹10.57 crore made under section 56(2)(vii)(c) after finding that the Assessing Officer wrongly adopted an amended valuation approach retrospectively. The Tribunal upheld the CIT(A)’s deletion in entirety. The post AO Cannot Apply Post-2018 “Look-Through” Valuation Retrospectively – ITAT Deletes Massive Section 56 Additions appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Mumbai ruled against the retrospective application of a post-2018 'look-through' valuation approach for Section 56(2)(vii)(c) additions. The Tribunal deleted additions exceeding ₹10.57 crore, affirming that amended valuation methodologies cannot be applied retroactively.

title: ITAT Deletes Software Cost Mark-Up Addition Holding Pure Reimbursement Is Not Profit-Making Service
summary: The ITAT Delhi ruled that reimbursement of software costs to foreign AEs on a cost-to-cost basis could not be treated as a profit-generating intra-group service. The Tribunal deleted the transfer pricing adjustment after finding the benchmarking method adopted by the TPO unjustified. The post ITAT Deletes Software Cost Mark-Up Addition Holding Pure Reimbursement Is Not Profit-Making Service appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Delhi ruled that pure reimbursement of software costs to foreign Associated Enterprises on a cost-to-cost basis should not be subject to a transfer pricing mark-up. The Tribunal deleted the transfer pricing adjustment, deeming the TPO’s benchmarking method for such non-profit-making services unjustified.

title: DCF Valuation Cannot Be Rejected Using Hindsight – ITAT Deletes ₹36.54 Crore Section 56(2)(viib) Addition
summary: ITAT Mumbai ruled that replacing projected cash flows with actual profits while applying the DCF method is legally impermissible. The decision reaffirmed that DCF valuation is inherently based on future estimates and business expectations. The post DCF Valuation Cannot Be Rejected Using Hindsight – ITAT Deletes ₹36.54 Crore Section 56(2)(viib) Addition appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Mumbai ruled that using hindsight to reject a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) valuation by replacing projected cash flows with actual profits is impermissible. The Tribunal deleted ₹36.54 crore additions under Section 56(2)(viib), reaffirming that DCF is inherently based on future estimates.

title: ITAT rejected TPO’s recharacterisation of Boeing India as Full-Risk entity in technical services arrangement
summary: Transfer Pricing Officer (TPO) had wrongly recharacterised Boeing India Defense Private Limited as a full-risk service provider despite the Associated Enterprise (AE) assuming the entire contractual and operational risks relating to defence support services rendered to the Indian Air Force (IAF). The post ITAT rejected TPO’s recharacterisation of Boeing India as Full-Risk entity in technical services arrangement appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT rejected the Transfer Pricing Officer's recharacterisation of Boeing India Defense Private Limited as a full-risk entity in its technical services arrangement. The Tribunal found that the Associated Enterprise, not Boeing India, assumed all contractual and operational risks for defense support services.

title: HMRC’s transfer pricing windfall: one-off or new normal? - Macfarlanes
summary: HMRC’s transfer pricing windfall: one-off or new normal? Macfarlanes
ai_summary: This article from Macfarlanes discusses whether HMRC's recent success in transfer pricing cases represents a temporary "windfall" or a sustained increase in enforcement. It analyzes the implications for multinational enterprises operating in the UK regarding their transfer pricing policies and compliance.

title: International Controlled Transactions Schedule (ICTS): a new UK transfer pricing reporting requirement for accounting periods commencing on or after 1 January 2027 - Saffery
summary: International Controlled Transactions Schedule (ICTS): a new UK transfer pricing reporting requirement for accounting periods commencing on or after 1 January 2027 Saffery
ai_summary: The UK is introducing a new International Controlled Transactions Schedule (ICTS) as a transfer pricing reporting requirement for accounting periods commencing on or after January 1, 2027. This signifies a major update to transfer pricing documentation and compliance obligations for multinational enterprises in the UK.

title: Amagi gets Rs 18 crore tax adjustment notice, to appeal transfer pricing order - Storyboard18
summary: Amagi gets Rs 18 crore tax adjustment notice, to appeal transfer pricing order Storyboard18
ai_summary: Amagi, an Indian company, has received a tax adjustment notice of Rs 18 crore related to a transfer pricing order. The company intends to appeal this order, indicating the beginning of a tax dispute regarding its international transactions.

title: Agricultural Land Also Covered by Section 56(2)(x) – ITAT Upholds Addition Based on DVO Valuation
summary: The Tribunal upheld tax addition where agricultural land was acquired below stamp duty valuation and DVO-determined fair market value. It ruled that agricultural status of land does not exclude applicability of section 56(2)(x). The post Agricultural Land Also Covered by Section 56(2)(x) – ITAT Upholds Addition Based on DVO Valuation appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT upheld a tax addition under Section 56(2)(x) where agricultural land was acquired below its stamp duty valuation and fair market value determined by the Departmental Valuation Officer (DVO). The Tribunal clarified that the agricultural status of the land does not exempt it from the applicability of this section.

title: ITAT Mumbai Excludes MPS Ltd. as Comparable, Deletes ₹18.80 Crore TP Adjustment in ITES Case
summary: ITAT Mumbai held that a company engaged in publishing platforms, software solutions, and product development could not be compared with a limited-risk captive ITES service provider. Exclusion of the comparable eliminated the transfer pricing adjustment. The post ITAT Mumbai Excludes MPS Ltd. as Comparable, Deletes ₹18.80 Crore TP Adjustment in ITES Case appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Mumbai has deleted a ₹18.80 crore transfer pricing adjustment in an ITES case by excluding MPS Ltd. as a comparable company. The Tribunal ruled that a company involved in publishing platforms, software solutions, and product development cannot be compared to a limited-risk captive ITES service provider.

title: Transfer Pricing Addition Sent Back as DRP Failed to Properly Examine Functional Comparability
summary: Tribunal found the DRP’s order cryptic and lacking proper analysis on similarity of business activities between the assessee and selected comparables. Fresh examination was directed regarding comparability and ALP computation. The post Transfer Pricing Addition Sent Back as DRP Failed to Properly Examine Functional Comparability appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Tribunal has remanded a transfer pricing addition for fresh examination, finding that the Dispute Resolution Panel (DRP) failed to properly analyze functional comparability between the assessee and selected comparables. The DRP's order was deemed cryptic and lacking a proper examination of business activity similarities.

title: SC LTC Ruling Cannot Retrospectively Create TDS Default: ITAT Ahmedabad
summary: ITAT ruled that although LTC exemption involving foreign travel stood disallowed by SC, such judgment could not retrospectively impose TDS default liability for periods covered by judicial interim protection. The post SC LTC Ruling Cannot Retrospectively Create TDS Default: ITAT Ahmedabad appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Ahmedabad ruled that a Supreme Court decision disallowing LTC exemption for foreign travel cannot retrospectively impose TDS default liability. The Tribunal clarified that such a judgment does not apply to periods covered by judicial interim protection.

title: No Double Taxation Allowed Even if Expense Claim Found Non-Genuine: ITAT Mumbai
summary: ITAT Mumbai held that although foreign commission expenditure was non-genuine and liable for disallowance, amounts already written back and taxed in a subsequent year could not again be taxed through disallowance in earlier years. The post No Double Taxation Allowed Even if Expense Claim Found Non-Genuine: ITAT Mumbai appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Mumbai ruled that even if a foreign commission expenditure claim is found non-genuine and disallowable, amounts already written back and taxed in a subsequent year cannot be taxed again. This decision reinforces the principle against double taxation for the same income.

title: ITAT Allows BAPA Margin for Non-US AE Transactions Due to Identical FAR Profile
summary: The Tribunal ruled that margins agreed under a Bilateral Advance Pricing Agreement may be used for non-covered AEs when transactions are functionally similar. The decision emphasized consistency and the lack of separate benchmarking by the TPO. The post ITAT Allows BAPA Margin for Non-US AE Transactions Due to Identical FAR Profile appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT ruled that margins agreed under a Bilateral Advance Pricing Agreement (BAPA) can be applied to non-US Associated Enterprise (AE) transactions if their Functional, Asset, and Risk (FAR) profiles are identical. This decision emphasizes consistency in transfer pricing application and was made in the absence of separate benchmarking by the TPO.

title: Customs Valuation Difference Alone Cannot Prove Undisclosed Purchases: ITAT Mumbai
summary: Mumbai ITAT observed that assessable value under customs law may differ from invoice value and such variation by itself cannot justify addition under Section 69C without further evidence. The post Customs Valuation Difference Alone Cannot Prove Undisclosed Purchases: ITAT Mumbai appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Mumbai ruled that a mere difference between assessable value under customs law and invoice value does not, by itself, justify an addition under Section 69C for undisclosed purchases. The Tribunal stated that further evidence is required to prove such undisclosed purchases.
